  1. org.jscience.core.methodology

Uses of Package
org.jscience.core.methodology

Packages that use org.jscience.core.methodology
Package
Description
org.jscience.core.methodology
Core functionality for the org.jscience.core.methodology package in the JScience library.
org.jscience.natural.chemistry.loaders
 
org.jscience.natural.chemistry.loaders.animl
Analytical Information Markup Language (AnIML) loader for JScience.
org.jscience.natural.methodology.experiment
Core functionality for the org.jscience.core.methodology.experiment package in the JScience library.
org.jscience.social.history.archeology
Provides classes and interfaces for archaeological research and analysis.
org.jscience.social.law
Provides the org.jscience.social.law module.
org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation
Core functionality for the org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation package in the JScience library.
org.jscience.social.sociology
- social structures, persons, and cultural modeling.
  • Classes in org.jscience.core.methodology used by org.jscience.core.methodology
    Class
    Description
    Experiment
    Represents a scientific experiment that takes an input configuration and produces a result.
    Hypothesis
    Represents a testable hypothesis.
    Observation
    Represents a captured data point during an experiment.
    Sample
    Represents a physical or conceptual sample used in a scientific experiment.
    ScientificField
    Extensible enumeration for scientific fields/disciplines.
  • Classes in org.jscience.core.methodology used by org.jscience.natural.chemistry.loaders
    Class
    Description
    ScientificTerm
    Represents a scientific term with its definition and source reference.
  • Classes in org.jscience.core.methodology used by org.jscience.natural.chemistry.loaders.animl
    Class
    Description
    Observation
    Represents a captured data point during an experiment.
    Sample
    Represents a physical or conceptual sample used in a scientific experiment.
    ScientificExperiment
    A robust, persistent implementation of the Experiment interface.

    Represents a scientific experiment that takes an input configuration and produces a result.

    A robust, persistent implementation of the Experiment interface.
